compiled_state.title: As a project being onboarded, I get a GitHub repo configured with CI workflow and Ruleset in one step
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Update the /dso:onboarding skill to configure a GitHub repository with the CI workflow and Ruleset during project setup.
**Why**: Onboarding is the entry point for new projects — it must produce a fully-enforced repo without manual GitHub UI steps.
**Scope**:
- IN: Bootstrap sequence (push CI workflow to main, then create Ruleset), integration into existing onboarding phases, pre-flight checks with manual guide fallback, first-time setup vs existing repo handling
- OUT: CI workflow authoring (Story 1), Ruleset script logic (Story 2), LLM review configuration (Story 3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running /dso:onboarding on a new project configures the GitHub repository with a CI workflow on main and an active Ruleset requiring all CI status checks
  ← Satisfies: "The /dso:onboarding skill configures the GitHub repository"
- When this story is complete, the bootstrap sequence merges the CI workflow file to main before creating the Ruleset, avoiding the chicken-and-egg deadlock
  ← Satisfies: "merges the CI workflow file to main first, then creates the Ruleset via gh api POST"
- When this story is complete, pre-flight failures (insufficient permissions, missing gh CLI) emit a manual setup guide and allow onboarding to continue without the Ruleset
  ← Satisfies: "emitting a manual setup guide and continuing without the Ruleset if either check fails"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Scope Overlap] The onboarding skill already has CI detection (Phase 1), CI questioning (Phase 2 area 5), and config writing (Phase 3). This story must integrate into these existing phases rather than creating a parallel GitHub repo configuration path. Specifically: the bootstrap sequence must fit into the existing Phase 3 hook installation step.
- [Security] Admin permissions are required for Ruleset creation. The pre-flight check must validate permissions before attempting the API call.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
